title: Error Handler Registration & Dispatch
activity_flow: --- PHP runtime triggers error → ErrorHandler::handle(int $level, string $message, string $file, int $line) ---
YES → return true  [error suppressed, silent exit]
IF true → append xdebug.scream notice to $message
DECISION: is $level E_WARNING or E_USER_WARNING AND message contains 'should either be used or intentionally ignored...'?
YES → outputWarning('Ignored new PHP warning…', outputEvenWithoutIO=true) → return true
NO  → return true  [no IO, silently ignore deprecation]
DECISION: STDERR constant defined and is a resource?
method_signatures: public static function register(?IOInterface $io = null): void
public static function handle(int $level, string $message, string $file, int $line): bool
private static function outputWarning(string $message, bool $outputEvenWithoutIO = false): void
key_decisions: @ operator check: (error_reporting() & $level) === 0 → suppress and return true
xdebug.scream: appends advisory note to message before further processing
New PHP version ignored warning: recognised by message substring, routed to outputWarning without throwing
Deprecation with no IO: silently ignored
Deprecation with IO, already seen + non-verbose: show 'More notices hidden' once (state 1→2), then suppress
outputWarning without IO: falls back to STDERR or echo only if outputEvenWithoutIO=true
state_variables: static ?IOInterface $io — set during register(), used in handle() and outputWarning()
static int<0,2> $hasShownDeprecationNotice — 0=none shown, 1=first shown, 2=truncation notice shown
key_relationships: ErrorHandler registered via PHP built-in set_error_handler()
ErrorHandler::register() called from Application.php (Composer\Console\Application) and test setUp()
error_types_handled: E_DEPRECATED / E_USER_DEPRECATED → deprecation path (log or silence)
E_WARNING / E_USER_WARNING with ignored-void message → outputWarning + suppress
All other non-suppressed errors → throw ErrorException
important_notes: Class is fully static; no instantiation. outputWarning is private. The @ operator suppression is detected via error_reporting() bitwise AND check — standard PHP mechanism. Verbose mode prints a filtered stack trace (only frames with file+line). The hasShownDeprecationNotice counter ensures the 'more notices hidden' message appears exactly once.
